'''Mark Johnson''' (born 28 February 1969) is a South African former [[rugby union]] and professional [[rugby league]] footballer who represented [[South Africa national rugby league team|South Africa]] in the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup]].

==Playing career==
Johnson originally played [[rugby union]] in South Africa before joining the [[London Broncos#1991–1994: London Crusaders|London Crusaders]], a [[rugby league]] club.<ref>[https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-preview-riley-holds-the-key-to-a-capital-crusade-1468337.html Riley holds the key to a capital crusade] ''The Independent'', 19 December 1993</ref>

Johnson went on to play for [[Workington Town]], [[Hull FC]] and the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]].<ref name=rlp/>

Between 1995 and 2000 Johnson played seven tests for [[South Africa national rugby league team|South Africa]].<ref name=rlp/>
